text: Titel (de) Titel (en) Students at Pondok Pesantren Nurul Amanah Bangkalan Gained Practical Knowledge and Confidence in Becoming Entrepreneurs Through The Community Enterprise Project based on ABCD Theory. Photo: Muhammad Fadhil The Program aimed at enhancing the economic capabilities of students in Pondok Pesantren Nurul Amanah Bangkalan (PONAB) and building their entrepreneurship skills. This program utilizes an Asset-Based Community Development (ABCD) theory which is a strategy and process that aims to empower local communities by analysing and maximizing their potentials, as well as using all the available assets within the community. The 10th and 11th-grade students of PONAB went through a series of training and methods where they first received theoretical lessons about Shariah mu’amalah (Islamic economic transactions) and then went on to learn the operation of various food stalls, a bottled water refilling station and an aquaculture business owned by PONAB. The increase in the students’ knowledge about Shariah mu’amalah after taking part in this entrepreneurship program reached 37% when compared to their scores before the pre-test and post-test that they went through. This proved that the students were able to recognize the opportunity of the market and increase their capabilities in dealing with business problems that follow the principles of Shariah Mu’amalah. By identifying the Pondok Pesantren’s physical and human resources as the opportunities and assets to be developed for increasing the students’ economy competence and empowering them to become independent, the PONAB entrepreneurship program shows the successful implementation of a pragmatic entrepreneurship approach which can be further developed as one of the approaches to increase the competitiveness of students in terms of employment and also as an alternative model to empower the economy of madrasa students in general.
explanation: Domain-specific details (organization, program activities, and a concrete 37% figure) and natural flow suggest human authorship, though polished phrasing could also be produced by AI.
